etymology.description: semantic 斗(斗) (dòu, "ladle") + phonetic 鬼(鬼) (guǐ) -> soup ladle -> leader
etymology.interpretation: The character originally described a large ladle or soup ladle, represented by the combination of 鬼(鬼) (guǐ), which supplies the sound, and 斗(斗) (dòu, "ladle"), which contributes the sense of a large measuring or serving tool. The idea of a large, commanding tool like a ladle is metaphorically extended to mean 'leader' in the sense of someone who stands out or is prominent.

examples.chinese: 他被选为团队的魁(他被選為團隊的魁)。
examples.pinyin: Tā bèi xuǎn wéi tuánduì de kuí.
examples.english: He was chosen as the leader of the team.

examples.chinese: 这场比赛的魁是个传奇(這場比賽的魁是個傳奇)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè chǎng bǐsài de kuí shì gè chuánqí.
examples.english: The leader of this competition is a legend.

examples.chinese: 领导者不一定是最大的魁(領導者不一定是最大的魁)。
examples.pinyin: Lǐngdǎozhě bù yídìng shì zuìdà de kuí.
examples.english: A leader is not necessarily the greatest.
